reference_answer: Authentication is done using tokens like personal access tokens or GitHub App tokens.
Using authenticated requests and avoiding excessive requests helps prevent rate limits.
Too many concurrent requests can trigger secondary rate limits.
A REST request includes method, path, headers, authentication, and parameters.
The list organization repositories endpoint returns repositories for a given organization.
Unauthenticated requests have lower rate limits and less access.
Webhooks are recommended instead of polling to avoid repeated requests.
Repository contents endpoints return file or directory contents.
Repository statistics endpoints provide data about repository activity.
The questions endpoint returns questions with filters like score and date.
GitHub supports personal tokens, app tokens, and GITHUB_TOKEN.
Polling should be avoided to reduce unnecessary API calls.
The answers endpoint returns undeleted answers.
Some fields require higher permissions to be visible.
Query parameters provide additional information for requests.
Docker is an open platform for packaging and running applications in isolated containers.
Docker images are built from a Dockerfile and are layered and reusable across environments.
Containers are isolated using Linux namespaces and cgroups.
Containers communicate via Docker networks, including the default bridge network and custom networks with DNS-based discovery.
Stateless means no session information is retained between requests on the server.
PUT replaces a resource entirely while PATCH applies partial modifications.
A 404 status code means the requested resource was not found.
HTTP headers convey metadata about requests and responses including content type, authentication, and caching.
Git was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005 as a distributed version control system.
Branches in Git are lightweight pointers to commits and are fast to create and switch between.
Fetching brings remote changes locally without merging, while pulling fetches and merges in one step.
The OpenAPI Specification describes HTTP API capabilities in a language-agnostic way for both humans and machines.
API endpoints are described in the paths object with operations per HTTP method including parameters and responses.
The components section holds reusable definitions referenced via $ref to avoid repetition across the specification.
Use requests.get() with the URL and optionally parameters and headers to send a GET request.
Use json.load() with an open file object or json.loads() with a JSON string to parse JSON in Python.
Virtual environments prevent dependency conflicts by giving each project its own isolated Python installation.
npm is the default package manager for Node.js used for installing and managing JavaScript packages.
package.json contains project metadata, dependencies, devDependencies, scripts, and the main entry point.
Statelessness means each REST request must contain all information needed to process it with no server-side session state.
Workflows can be triggered by push, pull_request, schedule, workflow_dispatch, and workflow_call events.
Workflow files use top-level YAML keys name, on, env, and jobs, where each job has a runs-on value and a steps list.
GitHub computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature of the payload and includes it in a header for the receiver to validate.
The major version is incremented when a breaking change incompatible with the previous API is introduced.
Use plural nouns for collections and unique identifiers for individual resources, with nesting limited to two levels.
Return an appropriate 4xx or 5xx status code with a structured body containing a machine-readable code and a human-readable message.
Cursor-based pagination uses an opaque token instead of offsets, giving stable results when items are inserted or deleted.
Clients send the ETag value in If-None-Match; if unchanged the server returns 304 Not Modified without resending the body.
For non-simple cross-origin methods the browser sends a preflight OPTIONS request to check server permissions before the real request.
OAuth 2.0 lets a third-party app obtain limited access to a user's resources via tokens without the user sharing their credentials.
PKCE prevents authorization code interception attacks for public clients that cannot securely store a client secret.
A JWT has three Base64URL-encoded parts: a header with the algorithm, a payload with claims, and a cryptographic signature.
The API returns 429 Too Many Requests with headers indicating the limit, remaining quota, and when the window resets.
The test pyramid recommends many unit tests, fewer integration tests, and few end-to-end tests to keep the suite fast and reliable.
An isolated unit test replaces real dependencies with mocks or stubs so only the unit under test is exercised.
Unit tests isolate the unit with test doubles; integration tests use real dependencies to verify component interactions.
TDD cycles through Red (write a failing test), Green (write the minimum code to pass), and Refactor (improve without breaking tests).
pytest discovers tests in files matching test_*.py or *_test.py where function names begin with test_.
A pytest fixture is a reusable setup component injected into tests by name, supporting scopes and teardown via yield.
Reviews under 400 lines of changed code are more effective at finding defects, making smaller and more frequent reviews preferable.
A commit subject should be 50 characters or fewer in the imperative mood with an optional body explaining what changed and why.
Rebase is preferred for local feature branches to produce a linear history but should not be used on shared branches.
Lightweight tags are simple commit pointers; annotated tags are full objects with a message, date, and tagger information.
A .gitignore file specifies files and directories Git should not track, such as build outputs, virtual environments, and secret files.
Logging sensitive data creates a security risk; best practices require omitting or masking passwords, tokens, and personal information.
Library code should call logging.getLogger(__name__) and avoid adding handlers, leaving logging configuration to the application.
Bare except clauses catch system-exiting exceptions like KeyboardInterrupt; specific exception types should be caught instead.
Python type hints have no runtime effect; they are used by static analysis tools such as mypy to catch type errors during development.
The @dataclass decorator generates __init__, __repr__, and __eq__ automatically from the class's type-annotated fields.
FastAPI generates OpenAPI documentation at /docs and /redoc automatically from route function signatures and Pydantic models.
FastAPI returns a 422 Unprocessable Entity response if a path parameter cannot be converted to its declared type.
FastAPI automatically returns 422 Unprocessable Entity with field-level details when request body data fails Pydantic validation.
Depends() declares a dependency that FastAPI resolves and injects into handlers, used for authentication, DB sessions, and shared config.
Pydantic raises a ValidationError with detailed field-level messages when input data does not match declared types or constraints.
JSON Schema describes the expected structure, types, required fields, and constraints of JSON documents and underpins OpenAPI schemas.
pyproject.toml is the modern standard replacing setup.py for defining Python project metadata, build system, and dependencies.
Run pip install -r requirements.txt to install all packages listed in the requirements file into the current environment.
Microservices decompose an application into independently deployable services per business capability; a monolith bundles everything into one unit.
An API gateway handles authentication, rate limiting, logging, SSL termination, and request routing on behalf of backend services.
Singletons introduce global state that makes tests interdependent; dependency injection allows the instance to be substituted in tests.
The Factory Method lets client code depend on an interface rather than a concrete class so new types can be introduced without modifying the client.
SRP states a class should have one reason to change, keeping it focused on one responsibility to avoid unrelated breakage.
Adding a new retrieval method by implementing BaseRetriever without changing the router applies OCP: extension without modification.
CI automatically builds and tests every change; CD additionally deploys every passing build to production automatically.
Docker Compose reads docker-compose.yml and starts all defined services with a single command, simplifying local multi-service development.
Hardcoding secrets in source code risks exposing them in version control; environment variables keep secrets outside the codebase.
The Divio system defines tutorials, how-to guides, explanations, and reference material as four distinct documentation types.
API documentation must include a getting-started guide, endpoint reference with schemas, error code explanations, and authentication instructions.
DI passes dependencies as arguments so tests can substitute fakes without modifying the class, enabling isolated testing.
REST clients call fixed endpoints returning predetermined shapes; GraphQL clients send a query specifying exactly the fields they need.
Secret scanning detects committed tokens and API keys using known patterns, notifies the owner, and can automatically revoke tokens for supported providers.
Dependabot monitors package manifests such as package.json and requirements.txt for new releases and for versions with known security vulnerabilities.
CodeQL models source code as a queryable database; code scanning runs CodeQL as a GitHub Actions workflow and annotates vulnerable lines in the repository.
GitHub Apps authenticate as themselves with scoped installation tokens installed per organisation or repository; OAuth Apps authenticate as a user with broader non-expiring token scopes.
GitHub Apps use short-lived scoped tokens, do not inherit all user permissions, and are the recommended type for automated workflows and third-party services.
GraphQL allows clients to specify exactly the nested fields they need in a single request, whereas REST returns fixed endpoint responses that may include unwanted data.
The gh auth login command handles authentication via browser-based OAuth or a personal access token and stores credentials securely.
Discussions are for open-ended community conversations such as ideas and Q&A, while Issues track specific bugs or tasks with a defined open/closed lifecycle.
In Q&A-category discussions, maintainers or the original poster can mark a reply as the accepted answer, resolving the thread visibly for other readers.
The Student Developer Pack provides free access to cloud credits, developer tools, and SaaS subscriptions from GitHub's partner companies at no cost.
GitHub Importer supports migrations from Subversion, Mercurial, and other Git hosts; the GitHub Enterprise Importer handles large-scale organisation migrations between GitHub products.
Sponsors make one-time or monthly recurring contributions at tiers set by the developer; GitHub waives its platform fee for the first year of the program.
The dependency graph maps all direct and transitive packages used by a repository from its manifest files, enabling vulnerability detection through Dependabot alerts.
A GitHub profile shows contribution history, pinned repositories, and a bio; accounts can be secured with two-factor authentication, SSH keys, and personal access tokens.
Push protection blocks commits containing known secret patterns before they enter the repository history, preventing the credential from ever being recorded in Git.

GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered assistant that helps developers write code faster by suggesting lines and functions based on context. It improves productivity, reduces manual work, and supports multiple programming languages.

GitHub Copilot may generate incorrect or insecure code and can introduce issues such as performance overhead or dependency on external services. Developers should carefully review, test, and validate all generated code and use Copilot as a supportive tool rather than relying on it completely.

When an issue is assigned to GitHub Copilot, it begins working on the task automatically and creates a pull request. After completing the task, it notifies developers so they can review and decide whether to accept or modify the changes.
Working in a local environment can improve development speed because it allows direct interaction with project files and provides faster feedback, enabling quicker implementation of changes.
Using conversational prompts reduces the learning curve because developers do not need to memorize complex commands and can instead express their intentions in natural language.
Before executing a task, Copilot CLI considers the current project state and environment to generate more relevant and accurate results.
DNS configuration is necessary to link a custom domain to GitHub Pages so that user requests are correctly directed to the hosted website.
GitHub Pages supports both apex domains and subdomains, allowing flexibility in how a website is accessed.
Serving content automatically through a custom domain ensures that users can access the website directly without relying on the default GitHub Pages URL.
GitHub Projects supports different views such as tables, boards, and roadmaps to help teams organize and track their work efficiently.
Issue fields in GitHub Projects can store structured data such as text, numbers, dates, and selection options, which help in organizing and managing tasks.
Reducing manual updates is important in large projects because manual tracking is time-consuming and error-prone, while automation keeps project boards accurate and consistently updated.
GitHub Projects helps teams monitor their work by providing a central view of all tasks, which makes it easier to track progress and identify ongoing activities.
Having a clear overview of tasks improves team productivity, where it allows team members to quickly understand priorities, identify pending work, and coordinate their efforts more efficiently.
The user can add special fields such as dates, status, priority, etc.
Adding fields makes task management easier and expends the context.
The application might resend the request when network issues prevent the response from reaching the server, even if the server successfully completed the operation.
Detecting recurring request helps to prevent the repeated execution of operations that should only occur once, thus reducing unexpected and unintended behavior.
Through the unique request id allow APIs to recognize repeated requests and reject duplicates which helps systems avoid processing the same operation more than once.
Safe filters are used to reduce the risk of injecting malicious content into web pages and to protect applications from script injection problems.
APIs return error details to help developers identify the cause of the problem and correct requests or handle errors appropriately.
Authentication and permission errors help prevent unauthorized access and ensure that operations are performed only by authorized users.
Permission partitioning helps reduce security risks by granting applications only the permissions they need to perform their tasks.
Ready-made JavaScript libraries help developers implement authentication easily by providing built-in functionality for managing login and obtaining access tokens.
Classic tokens grant broad scopes across all repositories. Fine-grained tokens scope permissions to specific repositories and resource types, reducing the blast radius if a token is leaked.
Generate a key pair with ssh-keygen, add the public key to GitHub under Settings > SSH and GPG keys, and use the SSH agent to authenticate. Ed25519 is recommended for new keys.
GitHub supports authenticator apps (TOTP), SMS, hardware security keys (FIDO2/WebAuthn), and GitHub Mobile as second factors.
Tokens should be issued with the minimum required scopes following the principle of least privilege. Unused scopes increase the potential damage if the token is exposed.

The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in CPython prevents more than one thread from executing Python bytecode at a time, so CPU-bound tasks do not run in parallel. The multiprocessing module or ProcessPoolExecutor should be used instead.
Users can review and revoke individual active sessions under Settings > Security. The security log records authentication events and OAuth app authorizations with timestamps and IP addresses.

GitFlow uses long-lived main and develop branches with feature, release, and hotfix branches. Trunk-based development keeps all developers on a single main branch with short-lived feature branches and feature flags to hide incomplete work.
Black reformats Python source files according to a single opinionated style. It is deterministic: the same input always produces the same output, and formatting is idempotent.

git stash saves tracked modifications to a stack and restores the working directory. git stash pop reapplies the most recent stash and removes it from the stack. git stash apply reapplies without removing.

A private security advisory lets maintainers discuss and coordinate a fix for a vulnerability before public disclosure, assign a CVE, and collaborate via a private fork without exposing the issue in the public fork graph.
Adding a SECURITY.md file enables the Report a vulnerability button on the Security tab and displays the policy to potential reporters so they know how to report issues responsibly.

CodeQL compiles source code into a queryable database representing the program's structure, data flow, and control flow. Queries are then run against this database to detect patterns matching known vulnerability classes.
GitHub Advanced Security includes code scanning powered by CodeQL, secret scanning with push protection, and dependency review in pull requests.

GitHub monitors the dependency graph built from manifest files. When a new advisory matching a used dependency version is published in the GitHub Advisory Database, an alert is automatically created for the repository.
Dependabot version updates are configured through a dependabot.yml file in the .github directory, specifying the package ecosystem, manifest directory, and update schedule.

Required status checks ensure that CI workflows such as tests and linting pass before a branch can be merged. Strict mode additionally requires the branch to be up to date with the base branch before the check is considered passing.
The GitHub Advisory Database feeds into Dependabot alerts, the dependency review action, and the GitHub REST and GraphQL APIs. It is also indexed by the National Vulnerability Database.

GitHub rejects the push and displays a message identifying the detected secret. The developer can bypass the block by providing a reason, but each bypass is logged and visible to repository administrators.
The security overview is available to organisation owners and security managers. It requires GitHub Advanced Security for private repositories.

GitHub Models provides a playground to test prompts against different models interactively, compare outputs side by side, and tune parameters such as temperature and system prompts before writing any code.

GitHub Models exposes an API endpoint compatible with the OpenAI client libraries. Authentication uses a GitHub personal access token, so only the base URL and API key need to change to redirect existing OpenAI SDK code.
A GitHub App authenticates using a JWT signed with its private key to obtain an installation access token. The token is short-lived and scoped to the specific installation.

When a GitHub App requests updated permissions, existing installations must accept the new permissions before the app can use them. GitHub notifies the account owner so they can review the change before approving.
Every webhook payload includes an action field describing what happened, a sender object identifying the triggering user, and a repository object describing where the event occurred.

A regular string comparison short-circuits as soon as a mismatch is found, allowing timing attacks that reveal how many bytes matched. A constant-time comparison always takes the same time regardless of where the mismatch occurs.
A mutation uses the mutation keyword and modifies data, while a query is read-only. Both return a defined payload type and are sent as POST requests to the same endpoint.
Use GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{ref} where ref is the commit SHA. The response includes the list of changed files with additions, deletions, and a patch for each file.
Use POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs with the branch name in the format refs/heads/branch-name and the SHA of the commit to branch from.
GET /user returns the authenticated user's login, name, email, bio, company, followers count, and public repository count, along with additional private fields accessible with the right token scopes.
Use GET /orgs/{org}/members/{username}, which returns 204 if the user is a member or 404 if not.

The search API is rate-limited to 30 requests per minute for authenticated requests because search queries are more expensive to execute than regular API calls, involving full-text indexing across the entire platform.

functools.wraps copies the wrapped function's __name__, __doc__, __qualname__, and __annotations__ to the wrapper function, ensuring introspection tools and debuggers see the original function's identity.
A class must implement __enter__ (called at the start of the with block, returns the context value) and __exit__ (called at the end, handles exceptions and performs cleanup).

A generator function uses yield instead of return. Calling it returns a generator object without executing the body. Execution resumes from the last yield on each call to next(), making it lazy and memory-efficient.

A list comprehension evaluates eagerly and stores all values in a list in memory. A generator expression uses parentheses and produces values lazily one at a time, without storing the entire sequence.

The with statement guarantees the file is closed after the block, even if an exception is raised, preventing resource leaks. Without it, a forgotten close() call or an unhandled exception can leave the file handle open.

pathlib overloads the / operator to join path segments, making path construction readable without nested function calls. For example, Path('dir') / 'sub' / 'file.txt' replaces os.path.join('dir', 'sub', 'file.txt').
defaultdict calls a factory function to supply the missing value instead of raising KeyError. For example, a defaultdict(list) automatically initialises a missing key to an empty list.
Named groups use the syntax (?P<name>...) and are retrieved from the match object using match.group('name'), making patterns more readable than positional group references.

datetime.utcnow() returns a naive datetime with no timezone information, which can be confused with local time. datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) returns an aware datetime that can be safely compared with other aware datetimes.
parser.parse_args() returns a Namespace object where each defined argument is accessible as an attribute, for example args.filename or args.verbose.

The WebSocket handshake starts as an HTTP/1.1 upgrade request with an Upgrade: websocket header and a Sec-WebSocket-Key. The server responds with 101 Switching Protocols, after which the connection switches to the WebSocket framing protocol.

A GraphQL schema defines the available types, fields, relationships, queries, mutations, and subscriptions using the Schema Definition Language. Requests are validated against the schema before execution.

Client-side validation can be bypassed by an attacker sending raw HTTP requests. Server-side validation is the only authoritative control and must enforce type, length, and allowed values for all inputs before they are used.

HTTP/1.1 head-of-line blocking means a slow response delays all subsequent responses on the same connection. HTTP/2 multiplexing interleaves multiple requests and responses on a single connection simultaneously, eliminating this bottleneck.
Contract testing verifies that an API implementation conforms to its published OpenAPI specification. Schemathesis can automatically generate and run test cases derived from the OpenAPI document.

Over-mocked tests can pass even when production code is broken because they verify mock interactions rather than real behaviour. Integration tests that use real dependencies should complement unit tests to ensure the system works end to end.
SSE is unidirectional: only the server can send messages to the client. WebSockets are full-duplex, allowing both sides to send messages at any time after the connection is established.

Duplication means a bug or requirement change must be fixed in multiple places. The DRY principle advocates extracting repeated logic into a shared abstraction so each piece of knowledge has a single authoritative representation.
Without tests it is difficult to verify that a structural change did not introduce a regression. Tests provide the safety net that allows refactoring to be performed in small, verifiable steps.

Divergent change occurs when a single class must be modified for multiple unrelated reasons. It suggests the class has too many responsibilities and should be split so each part has a single reason to change.

Producers publish events to a broker without knowing which consumers will process them. Consumers subscribe to event types independently. Neither side needs a direct reference to the other, enabling independent scaling and replacement.
If a consumer crashes before acknowledging a message, the broker redelivers the message to another consumer, providing at-least-once delivery semantics and ensuring the message is not lost.

In cache-aside, the application checks the cache first; on a miss it fetches data from the source, stores it in the cache, and serves it. Subsequent requests for the same key are served from the cache.

Parameterised queries separate SQL code from data by sending the query structure and parameters independently. The database driver handles escaping, preventing user input from being interpreted as SQL commands.
Intentional debt is a deliberate shortcut taken to meet a deadline with a plan to refactor later. Unintentional debt arises from lack of skill or knowledge and may not be recognised until much later.

A canary release shifts only a small percentage of traffic to the new version. Error rates and performance are monitored on the canary population before rolling out to all users, limiting the blast radius of any bug.
Configuration should be stored in environment variables, not in the codebase, so the same build artifact can run in development, staging, and production without modification.
The three pillars of software observability are logs (timestamped event records), metrics (numeric measurements aggregated over time), and traces (distributed request tracking across services).

Renaming a column in one step breaks running application instances that still reference the old name. A multi-step migration adds the new column, backfills data, updates the application to use both names, then removes the old column after all instances are updated.
Bandit detects patterns associated with hardcoded passwords, use of insecure functions like eval(), weak cryptographic configurations, and other security anti-patterns in Python source code.

The four Agile Manifesto values are: individuals and interactions over processes and tools; working software over comprehensive documentation; customer collaboration over contract negotiation; and responding to change over following a plan.

Storing infrastructure as code enables version control, code review, and consistent recreation of environments from configuration files alone, eliminating configuration drift caused by manual changes and ensuring all environments are identical.
When a metric becomes a target, developers optimise the metric rather than the underlying quality it was meant to measure. For example, high test coverage does not guarantee meaningful tests.
Document stores such as MongoDB store data as JSON-like documents grouped in collections. Each document can have a different structure, suiting data with variable or evolving schemas.
Running as a non-root user reduces the attack surface: if the container process is compromised, the attacker does not gain root privileges on the host system.

functools.lru_cache has a maxsize parameter to limit the number of cached entries and evicts the least recently used entry when full. functools.cache is equivalent to lru_cache(maxsize=None), creating an unbounded cache.

Each layer can be independently tested and replaced. The business logic layer can be unit tested without a database by substituting the data access layer with a fake, and the data access layer can change storage backends without altering business logic.
os.getenv returns a default value if the variable is not set, avoiding a KeyError. Accessing os.environ directly raises KeyError for missing keys unless .get() is used.
Pass the endCursor from the previous page's pageInfo as the after argument in the next query, along with first to specify how many items to return. Repeat until hasNextPage is false.

A lock file records the exact resolved versions of all direct and transitive dependencies, ensuring that all developers and CI environments use identical dependency trees and producing reproducible builds.

sys.path is a list of directories Python searches when importing modules. Python checks each directory in order until the module is found. Appending a directory to sys.path makes modules in that directory importable.

Rulesets support flexible branch and tag pattern targeting and can be applied across multiple repositories or an entire organisation from a single configuration. Branch protection rules are per-repository and do not support organisation-wide targeting or evaluate mode.

itertools.groupby requires the input to be sorted by the key function because it only groups consecutive elements with the same key. If the input is unsorted, the same key may appear in multiple separate groups.
A devcontainer.json file in the repository configures the Codespace, specifying the base image, VS Code extensions, port forwarding, environment variables, and setup scripts.

Code-first tools like FastAPI derive documentation directly from route function signatures and Pydantic models, so documentation is updated automatically whenever the code changes, eliminating manual synchronisation.

GitHub builds the dependency graph by parsing manifest and lock files such as requirements.txt, package.json, and pom.xml in the repository, recording each direct and transitive dependency and its version.
Python raises TypeError when you attempt to instantiate a subclass of an ABC that has not implemented all abstract methods, enforcing the interface at class definition time.
GitHub Enterprise Cloud adds SAML single sign-on, SCIM provisioning from an identity provider, audit log streaming to SIEM systems, and IP allow lists to restrict access to known network ranges.

Enums prevent typos because misspelled strings are not valid members, enable IDE autocompletion, and make the set of valid values explicit in the code, unlike bare strings or integers that can hold any value.
